_meta.full_name: IPIP-HEXACO Scales — International Personality Item Pool representation of the HEXACO personality model (Ashton, Lee, & Goldberg, 2007) (Korean translation)
_meta.license: Public domain. Per the official IPIP website (ipip.ori.org): "The items and scales are in the public domain, which means that one can copy, edit, translate, or use them for any purpose without asking permission and without paying a fee." The IPIP was created by Lewis R. Goldberg with NIMH support; no permission or fee is required for any use, commercial or non-commercial.
_meta.reuse_notes: 한국어 번역(파생물). 비상업 1단계용. LLM 번역이며 검증된 한국어 표준검사가 아님 — 실사용 전 재검수 권장. 라이선스는 원본을 따르되 번역은 파생물이므로 CC-SA/연구용 조건이 그대로 적용됨.
_meta.attribution: Items authored/compiled by Lewis R. Goldberg and the International Personality Item Pool (IPIP; https://ipip.ori.org). Scale construction: Ashton, M. C., Lee, K., & Goldberg, L. R. (2007). The IPIP-HEXACO scales: An alternative, public-domain measure of the personality constructs in the HEXACO model. Personality and Individual Differences, 42, 1515-1526. The IPIP representations were built to parallel the constructs of Lee & Ashton's (2004) HEXACO Personality Inventory. Recommended IPIP citation: Goldberg, L. R. (1999) and Goldberg et al. (2006), Journal of Research in Personality, 40, 84-96. · 한국어 번역: deepinme 번역 워크플로우(2026-07, LLM).
_meta.scoring_notes: Structure: 6 HEXACO factors, each with 4 facet scales (24 facets total), each facet measured by exactly 10 items = 240 items. Factor -> facet map with IPIP abbreviations and Cronbach's alpha reported on the source key page (from Ashton, Lee, & Goldberg, 2007): HONESTY-HUMILITY {Sincerity (H:Sinc) a=.81, Fairness (H:Fair) a=.77, Greed Avoidance (H:Gree) a=.69, Modesty (H:Mode) a=.81}; EMOTIONALITY {Fearfulness (E:Fear) a=.84, Anxiety (E:Anxi) a=.85, Dependence (E:Depe) a=.73 (all 10 items +keyed), Sentimentality (E:Sent) a=.79}; EXTRAVERSION {Expressiveness (X:Expr) a=.84, Social Boldness (X:SocB) a=.86, Sociability (X:Soci) a=.85, Liveliness (X:Live) a=.82}; AGREEABLENESS {Forgiveness (A:Forg) a=.78, Gentleness (A:Gent) a=.81, Flexibility (A:Flex) a=.73, Patience (A:Pati) a=.88}; CONSCIENTIOUSNESS {Organization (C:Orga) a=.85, Diligence (C:Dili) a=.81, Perfectionism (C:Perf) a=.80, Prudence (C:Prud) a=.80}; OPENNESS TO EXPERIENCE {Aesthetic Appreciation (O:AesA) a=.83, Inquisitiveness (O:Inqu) a=.78, Creativity (O:Crea) a=.85, Unconventionality (O:Unco) a=.84}. Keying: keyed="+" items score in the direction of the facet; keyed="-" items are reverse-scored before summing. On the customary 5-point scale (1=Very Inaccurate ... 5=Very Accurate) reverse scoring is 5->1, 4->2, 3->3, 2->4, 1->5. Overall balance across the 240 items: 118 positively keyed, 122 negatively keyed. Items are first-person self-report statements administered with a standard IPIP instruction stem ("Describe yourself as you generally are now... indicate how accurately each statement describes you").
_meta.cautions: 1) This is the FULL 240-item facet-level IPIP-HEXACO (24 facet scales x 10 items), fetched verbatim from the official IPIP key page. The task cited "the 100-item IPIP-HEXACO" only as an example; the authoritative IPIP site hosts this complete 240-item facet version, so the entire coherent set was collected rather than a partial subset. All 240 items were captured (none omitted); a shorter 100-item or 60-item public-domain HEXACO subset can be derived from these same items if needed. 2) The "num" field is the sequential position within the source key listing (ordered factor -> facet -> +keyed then -keyed), NOT an official IPIP master-item identifier; IPIP assigns its own item IDs in its 3,000+ alphabetical item pool. 3) These public-domain IPIP items are an ALTERNATIVE proxy for the HEXACO constructs; they are NOT the copyrighted HEXACO-PI-R by Lee & Ashton. The proprietary HEXACO-PI-R (hosted at hexaco.org) was deliberately NOT reproduced here; only the public-domain IPIP representation was collected. 4) Reported alpha reliabilities are from the 2007 development sample and are provided on the source page for reference. 5) Item text is reproduced exactly as it appears on the IPIP key page (including punctuation and quotation marks); these are stage-1 raw items intended to be rewritten into plain language downstream. | 번역본: 문항 순서·채점방향(keyed)·factor/facet은 원본과 동일. text_en에 원문 병기.

translation.method: LLM faithful translation (deepinme workflow, 2026-07) — 충실 번역이며 '쉬운말 리라이팅'은 별도 단계
translation.status: working draft — NOT a validated Korean instrument

qa.reviewer_overall: All 240 items are present and sequential (count matches), one Korean rendering per source item. Fidelity is high: measured meaning and first-person self-report framing are preserved, reverse-keyed items are translated literally rather than un-reversed (e.g., 22 '애쓰지 않는다', 189 '너무 기울이지 않는다', 227 '좋지 않다'), and consistent terminology is used across sibling items (e.g., 화/짜증/겁). Naturalness is strong — the items read as idiomatic Korean questionnaire statements, with good idiomatic choices for tricky items (82 '할 말이 없어 막히는 법이 없다', 150 '도무지 말이 통하지 않는다', 235 '세상의 흐름을 거슬러 나아간다'), and little to no translationese. Only three minor points surfaced: a semantic nuance shift on id 10 ('feel important' → '우월감/superiority'), a case-particle slip on id 172 ('일을 착수한다'), and a mild narrowing on id 55 ('panic' → '겁에 질린다'). A handful of items add small intensifiers/explicitations (e.g., 30 '오로지', 61 '안심시켜 주는 말', 158 '사소한 일에도', 200 '남들 앞에서') that are defensible and do not distort the construct. No true mistranslations or framing errors were found.

issues.problem: 'Let people push me around to help them feel important' — 'feel important' is rendered as '우월감을 느끼도록' (feel superiority), which shifts the meaning from others feeling valued/significant to others feeling superior to me. The rest ('함부로 대하게 내버려 둔다') is good.
issues.suggestion: 남들이 자신이 중요한 사람이라고 느끼도록 그들이 나를 함부로 대하게 내버려 둔다.

issues.problem: 'Get started quickly on doing a job' → '일을 빨리 착수한다' uses the wrong case particle: 착수하다 governs 에 (일에 착수하다), so 일을 착수한다 reads as slightly ungrammatical 번역투.
issues.suggestion: 일에 빨리 착수한다 (or more naturally: 일을 빨리 시작한다).

issues.problem: 'Panic easily' → '쉽게 겁에 질린다' narrows 'panic' to fear/terror (질리다 = be terrified), losing the agitation / loss-of-composure sense. Minor; also overlaps semantically with item 42's '겁에 질리기'.
issues.suggestion: 쉽게 당황한다 (or 쉽게 허둥댄다 / 쉽게 공황에 빠진다).

verification.summary: 번역 QA(2차 에이전트): fidelity=high, naturalness=natural, count_match=True, issues=3

sources.title: IPIP-HEXACO key page: The Items in the Preliminary IPIP Scales Measuring Constructs Similar to Those Included in Lee and Ashton's HEXACO Personality Inventory (official IPIP / Oregon Research Institute)
International Personality Item Pool (IPIP) home page — public-domain statement and site overview

Ashton, Lee, & Goldberg (2007), The IPIP-HEXACO scales: An alternative, public-domain measure of the personality constructs in the HEXACO model, Personality and Individual Differences, 42, 1515-1526 (author PDF)
Korean translation (deepinme workflow)
sources.license: translation is a derivative; see reuse_notes

items.text: 실제 나보다 더 나은 사람인 척하지 않는다.
items.text_en: Don't pretend to be more than I am.

items.text: 남들이 내가 원하는 대로 행동하게 하려고 그들이 듣고 싶어 하는 말을 해 준다.
items.text_en: Tell other people what they want to hear so that they will do what I want them to do.

items.text: 남들에게 강한 인상을 주려고 겉으로 그럴싸하게 꾸민다.
items.text_en: Put on a show to impress people.
Switch my loyalties when I feel like it.

items.text: 남들에게 강한 인상을 주려고 어떤 역할을 연기한다.
items.text_en: Play a role in order to impress people.
Pretend to be concerned for others.
Act like different people in different situations.

items.text: 권력을 가진 사람들의 비위를 맞출 필요가 있다고 느낀다.
items.text_en: Find it necessary to please the people who have power.

items.text: 남들이 우월감을 느끼도록 그들이 나를 함부로 대하게 내버려 둔다.
items.text_en: Let people push me around to help them feel important.

items.text: 내 것이 아닌 물건은 절대 가져가지 않는다.
items.text_en: Would never take things that aren't mine.
Would never cheat on my taxes.

items.text: 계산원이 실수로 거스름돈을 더 주면 돌려준다.
items.text_en: Return extra change when a cashier makes a mistake.

items.text: 누군가의 물건을 훔친다면 오랫동안 몹시 죄책감을 느낄 것이다.
items.text_en: Would feel very badly for a long time if I were to steal from someone.

items.text: 앞서 나가기 위해 부정한 수단을 쓴다.

items.text_en: Cheat on people who have trusted me.

items.text: 충동적으로 누군가를 이용한다 해도 내 행동을 후회하지 않을 것이다.
items.text_en: Would not regret my behavior if I were to take advantage of someone impulsively.

items.text: 유명 인사가 되는 것을 즐기지 않을 것이다.
items.text_en: Would not enjoy being a famous celebrity.
Don't strive for elegance in my appearance.
Have a strong need for power.
Prefer to eat at expensive restaurants.
Am out for my own personal gain.
Don't think that I'm better than other people.
See myself as an average person.

items.text: 남들보다 더 많은 권력을 갖고 싶다.
items.text_en: Would like to have more power than other people.
Believe that I am better than others.
Am more capable than most others.
Am likely to show off if I get the chance.
Begin to panic when there is danger.

items.text: 범죄가 많은 도시 지역을 걷는 것이 두려울 것 같다.
items.text_en: Would fear walking in a high-crime part of a city.

items.text: 카누를 타고 급류를 내려가는 일은 절대 하지 않을 것이다.
items.text_en: Would never go riding down a stretch of rapids in a canoe.

items.text: 불타는 건물에서 사람들을 구조하는 일을 잘할 것이다.
items.text_en: Would be good at rescuing people from a burning building.

items.text: 결국 별것 아닌 일로 자주 걱정한다.
items.text_en: Often worry about things that turn out to be unimportant.

items.text: 머릿속에 떠오르는 불쾌한 생각들 때문에 마음이 상한다.
items.text_en: Get upset by unpleasant thoughts that come into my mind.
Am not easily disturbed by events.
Don't worry about things that have already happened.
Let myself be influenced by others.

items.text: 슬플 때 내 표정에 그 감정이 드러나는 것 같다.
items.text_en: Suspect that my facial expressions give me away when I feel sad.

items.text: 다른 사람들과 어울리지 않고는 지낼 수 없다.
items.text_en: Can't do without the company of others.

items.text: 안 좋은 일을 전해 들으면 곧바로 슬퍼진다.
items.text_en: Immediately feel sad when hearing of an unhappy event.

items.text: 다른 사람이 무엇을 필요로 하는지 민감하게 알아차린다.
items.text_en: Am sensitive to the needs of others.

items.text: 다른 사람의 불행에 깊이 마음이 움직인다.
items.text_en: Am deeply moved by others' misfortunes.

items.text: 슬픈 영화를 봐도 좀처럼 울지 않는다.
이야기의 슬픈 대목을 읽어도 좀처럼 눈물이 나지 않는다.
items.text_en: Seldom feel weepy while reading the sad part of a story.

items.text: 낯선 사람이 겪는 고통을 봐도 좀처럼 마음이 쓰이지 않는다.
items.text_en: Am seldom bothered by the apparent suffering of strangers.
Don't understand people who get emotional.

items.text: 할 말이 없어 막히는 법이 없다.
items.text_en: Am never at a loss for words.
Am the life of the party.

items.text: 짜증이 날 때는 그 사실을 남들에게 말한다.
items.text_en: Tell people about it when I'm irritated.

items.text: 나에게 이목이 쏠리는 것을 좋아하지 않는다.
items.text_en: Don't like to draw attention to myself.
Am good at making impromptu speeches.

items.text: 주목의 중심에 서는 것을 개의치 않는다.
items.text_en: Don't mind being the center of attention.

items.text: 사람들 앞에서 연설하는 것이 두려울 것 같다.
items.text_en: Would be afraid to give a speech in public.
Find it difficult to approach others.

items.text: 주목의 중심에 서는 것을 몹시 싫어한다.
items.text_en: Hate being the center of attention.

items.text: 여가 시간을 대개 사람들과 함께 보내는 것을 좋아한다.
items.text_en: Usually like to spend my free time with people.

items.text: 파티에서 여러 다른 사람들과 이야기를 나눈다.
items.text_en: Talk to a lot of different people at parties.
Enjoy being part of a group.

items.text: 다른 사람들만큼 사람들과 어울리는 데서 즐거움을 느끼지 못하는 것 같다.
items.text_en: Seem to derive less enjoyment from interacting with people than others do.

items.text: 사람들과 함께 있는 것을 좀처럼 즐기지 않는다.
사람들과 많이 교류해야 하는 일은 즐기지 못할 것이다.
items.text_en: Would not enjoy a job that involves a lot of social interaction.

items.text: 나는 남들이 쉽게 알기 어려운 사람이다.
items.text_en: Am hard to get to know.
Maintain high energy throughout the day.
Am usually active and full of energy.

items.text: 대부분의 시간 동안 건강하고 활기차다고 느낀다.
items.text_en: Feel healthy and vibrant most of the time.

items.text: 내 안에 강한 힘이 있다고 느낀다.
items.text_en: Feel that I have a lot of inner strength.

items.text: 화를 내야 마땅한 사람에게도 친절하게 대한다.
items.text_en: Am nice to people I should be angry at.
Find it hard to forgive others.
Get back at people who insult me.

items.text: 대부분의 사람은 믿을 수 없다고 느낀다.
items.text_en: Feel that most people can't be trusted.
Have a good word for everyone.

items.text: 남들이 내 기대에 미치지 못하면 답답하고 화가 난다.
items.text_en: Become frustrated and angry with people when they don't live up to my expectations.

items.text: 여러 사람과 함께 있을 때 그중 적어도 한 명은 거슬릴 때가 많다.
items.text_en: When interacting with a group of people, am often bothered by at least one of them.

items.text: 내가 정리해 둔 방식을 남이 바꾸면 언짢아진다.
items.text_en: Get upset if others change the way that I have arranged things.

items.text: 누군가에게 화가 나기까지는 꽤 많은 일이 있어야 한다.
items.text_en: Find that it takes a lot to make me feel angry at someone.

items.text: 모든 것이 "딱 맞게" 되어 있기를 바란다.

items.text_en: Leave a mess in my room.
Don't finish the things that I start.

items.text: 물건을 제자리에 도로 놓는 것을 자주 잊는다.
items.text_en: Often forget to put things back in their proper place.

items.text: 성공하기 위해 나 자신을 몹시 몰아붙인다.
items.text_en: Push myself very hard to succeed.
Get started quickly on doing a job.
Do just enough work to get by.
Stop when work becomes too difficult.
Quickly lose interest in the tasks I start.

items.text: 모든 세부 사항이 빠짐없이 처리되기를 바란다.
items.text_en: Want every detail taken care of.
Want everything to add up perfectly.

items.text: 세부 사항에 주의를 너무 기울이지 않는다.
items.text_en: Pay too little attention to details.

items.text: 그냥 일이 되어 가는 대로 두는 편을 선호한다.
items.text_en: Prefer to just let things happen.
Make plans and stick to them.
Do things according to a plan.
Do things without thinking of the consequences.

items.text: 무언가 마음에 걸릴 때 충동적으로 행동한다.
items.text_en: Act impulsively when something is bothering me.

items.text: 내가 하는 행동 중 일부는 왜 그러는지 나도 모른다.
items.text_en: Don't know why I do some of the things I do.

items.text: 남들 앞에서 바보 같은 짓을 한다.

items.text_en: Believe in the importance of art.

items.text: 남들이 미처 알아차리지 못하는 것에서 아름다움을 발견한다.
items.text_en: See beauty in things that others might not notice.
Have read the great literary classics.

items.text: 그림이나 사진의 정서적인 면을 좀처럼 알아차리지 못한다.
items.text_en: Seldom notice the emotional aspects of paintings and pictures.

items.text: 무용 공연을 보는 것을 즐기지 않는다.
items.text_en: Do not enjoy watching dance performances.
Would love to explore strange places.

items.text: 어려운 글을 읽는 것을 아주 좋아한다.
정치·사회 문제에 대해 굳이 신경 쓰지 않는다.
items.text_en: Don't bother worrying about political and social problems.
Will not probe deeply into a subject.

items.text: 대화를 더 수준 높은 방향으로 이끌어 간다.
items.text_en: Carry the conversation to a higher level.

items.text: 무언가를 하는 새로운 방법을 궁리해 내기를 아주 좋아한다.
items.text_en: Love to think up new ways of doing things.
Do not have a good imagination.
Have trouble guessing how others will react.

items.text: 갑작스러운 직관적 통찰을 좀처럼 경험하지 못한다.

items.text_en: Am considered to be kind of eccentric.

items.text: 내 생각이 때때로 남들을 놀라게 한다는 것을 안다.
items.text_en: Know that my ideas sometimes surprise people.
Do things that others find strange.

items.text: 별나거나 이상한 사람으로 여겨지는 것을 몹시 싫어할 것이다.
items.text_en: Would hate to be considered odd or strange.
Enjoy being thought of as a normal "mainstream" person.
Like to be viewed as proper and conventional.
Like to be thought of as a normal kind of person.
